headline: Roofing Reality Check: Risk Is Rising Even in Quiet Storm Years
summary: Hail volatility, aging roofs drive higher residential replacement severity, Verisk findsJERSEY CITY, N.J., May 29, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Even a near 20 percent decline in overall claims volume in 2025 couldn’t slow rising roof losses. The 2026 Verisk U.S. Roof Report shows average U.S. residential replacement costs jumped 33 percent and repair costs climbed 25 percent in 2025 compared to the prior four-year average.
